statistic_type: Nickel Directive post-assembly content limit as described in source
quote_trace: The source describes Directive 94/27/EC as prohibiting nickel in post assemblies during epithelization unless homogeneous and nickel concentration is less than 0.05% m/m.

statistic_type: Nickel Directive release-rate limit as described in source
quote_trace: The source describes Directive 94/27/EC as applying a 0.5 µg/cm2/week nickel-release limit to products in direct and prolonged contact with the skin.
The source recommends replacing the Nickel Directive post-assembly nickel-content requirement with a nickel migration limit for all post assemblies of 0.2 µg/cm2/week using EN 1811.

The source states that for finished stainless steel wires and commercial stainless steel piercing post assemblies, nickel release could not be measured because values were below the instrumentation detection limit of 0.01 µg/cm2/week.
No food, infant formula, or baby-food concentrations are measured in this source; Category 1 nickel food occurrence cells remain data gaps until food-matrix sources are ingested.
